weather.summary: Saturday: 17°C, overcast clouds. Sunday: 12°C, light rain

metadata.greeting: This weekend is packed with exciting activities for your little one! I've found a mix of indoor and outdoor adventures, from free craft sessions to animal encounters and theatre shows. With a bit of sunshine on Saturday and a touch of rain on Sunday, there's something for every weather!

activities.title: Library Club at the Hackney Showroom
activities.description: Reading corner and diverse book-pairing service for children of all ages.
activities.whyItWorks: A free, relaxed space to foster a love of reading. Perfect for little ones to discover new stories and enjoy quiet time.

transport.fromHackney: Various buses from Hackney Central to Hackney Town Hall, short walk to Hackney Showroom
transport.tip: Check Hackney Showroom website for term dates.

activities.title: Two weeks of Easter activities in Victoria Park
activities.description: A full fortnight of free, daily activities that stretch from sports and fishing on Tuesday March 31 through to pond dipping on Friday April 10. There's a break for the Easter weekend but otherwise there are one to two major activities a day.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of space to run around, variety of activities, and fresh air!

transport.fromHackney: Buses from Hackney to Victoria Park.
transport.tip: Check the Victoria Park Facebook page for a schedule, details of start points and the activities you do have to book for

activities.title: Mudlarks Family Gallery
activities.description: Fun filled zone where children can bring their imaginations to life with a DLR train and interactive water table.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of hands-on activities designed for children, space to move and explore.

transport.fromHackney: Jubilee Line from Stratford to Canary Wharf.
transport.tip: Visit during off-peak hours for a less crowded experience.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: Two floors of immersive play space known as the Story Worlds as well as the Story Garden playground.
activities.whyItWorks: Immersive play, caters well for little children, story themed soft play area.

transport.fromHackney: Overground from Hackney Wick to Stratford.
transport.tip: Check the website for specific event times and age recommendations.

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: Interactive galleries, called Design, Imagine and Play all of which contain an extensive collection of objects relating to childhood including over 2000 toys. There's an under 2s space known as the Mini Museum which was a sensory explosion for babies and toddlers. The museum runs classes such as Sound Explorers and Mini Play for babies and toddlers
activities.whyItWorks: Specifically designed with children in mind, plenty to keep them entertained, Mini Museum for under 2s for sensory experience.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line from Bethnal Green
transport.tip: Avoid peak hours for a less crowded experience.

activities.title: Library Club at the Hackney Showroom
activities.description: Reading corner and diverse book-pairing service for children of all ages.
activities.whyItWorks: A free, relaxed space to foster a love of reading. Perfect for little ones to discover new stories and enjoy quiet time.

transport.fromHackney: Various buses from Hackney Central to Hackney Town Hall, short walk to Hackney Showroom
transport.tip: Check Hackney Showroom website for term dates.

activities.title: Family Craft Saturdays
activities.description: Family craft activities inspired by the Museum's collections. This week craft an activity to take around the gardens and explore our Nature Trail.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on, creative, and explores nature. The gardens offer space to run around afterwards.

transport.fromHackney: Overground from Highbury & Islington to Forest Hill.
transport.tip: Arrive early as it's drop-in and can get busy.

activities.title: Spring Family Fun Festival
activities.description: A festival of fun and creativity for families including shows, musical adventures, puppetry and free activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of choice, something for all ages, mix of free and paid activities.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Liverpool Street, then Central line to Waterloo.
transport.tip: Check the Southbank Centre website for the full schedule and booking details.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore the natural world from dinosaurs to insects. The museum is running an Easter Family Festival from April 5 to April 20, with free family-friendly activities
activities.whyItWorks: Lots to see and discover, dinosaurs are always a hit! Free entry makes it an affordable day out.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria line to Green Park, then Piccadilly line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Visit early to avoid crowds. Check website for Family Festival details.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Explore the wonders of science and technology with interactive exhibits. Space Toilet Tales, an opportunity to set on a replica space toilet and ask an expert any question that might come to mind about the practicalities of going to the bathroom in orbit
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on exhibits, engaging for all ages, and free entry. The Garden is an interactive space for younger children to discover construction, water, light and sound through play.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria line to Green Park, then Piccadilly line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: The Garden is open Tues–Sun. Book tickets for special exhibitions in advance.

activities.title: British Library - Fairy Tales Exhibition
activities.description: This immersive family exhibition invites children to step into the magical world of classic fairy tales through beautiful illustrations, pop-up books, original manuscripts and even puppets. Interactive elements throughout including a magic mirror, a genie and even magical potions
activities.whyItWorks: Immersive environment, caters well for children who love storytelling.

transport.fromHackney: Overground from Highbury & Islington to Euston.
transport.tip: Check the British Library website for specific times and events. Booking online is required.

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: A small zoo in the Hampstead Heath area, home to rare species of birds and animals. They've got wallabies, lemurs, Scottish wildcat and more!
activities.whyItWorks: Free entry, animals to see, and a park to run around in.

transport.fromHackney: Northern Line from Finsbury Park to Golders Green.
transport.tip: Combine with a walk on Hampstead Heath for a full day out.

activities.title: Hyde Park
activities.description: Ideal for Easter walks, picnics, and family games. The park's Serpentine lake, open lawns, and blooming flower beds provide a stunning backdrop for seasonal photos. Children can enjoy playgrounds
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of space to run around, playgrounds, and fresh air. The Serpentine offers opportunities to see ducks and other wildlife.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Lancaster Gate or Marble Arch.
transport.tip: Pack a picnic to enjoy by the Serpentine.

activities.title: Vauxhall City Farm
activities.description: Running free Easter hunts and bonnet-making crafts from March 28 to April 12. A host of goats, sheep and smaller animals right on your doorstep.
activities.whyItWorks: Animals, hands-on activities, and free entry. A great way to experience farm life in the city.

transport.fromHackney: Victoria Line to Vauxhall.
transport.tip: Check for special events during the Easter period.

activities.title: Diana Memorial Playground
activities.description: Most of the action takes place around the enormous Peter Pan-inspired pirate ship and surrounding beach, but there's also a sensory trail, teepees and plenty of seating for parents to rest their weary legs!
activities.whyItWorks: Great play space, sensory trail, teepees, and a pirate ship!

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Notting Hill Gate, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Can get busy, so arrive early or visit during off-peak times.

activities.title: Hamleys Easter Happenings
activities.description: Lots of fun Easter happenings at London's biggest toy shop including Chef Chocco's Chocolatiers Puppet Show, Hamley's Rainbow ribbon parade, Storytime, Hamley's Dragon Easter egg hunt… and more!
activities.whyItWorks: Puppet show, parades, storytime and egg hunt.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Liverpool Street, then Central line to Oxford Circus.
transport.tip: Check the Hamleys website for the schedule and booking details.

activities.title: Hobbledown Heath Easter Egg'splorers
activities.description: Follow the themed egg trail, climb and explore across indoor and outdoor play, and meet animals along the way.
activities.whyItWorks: Egg trail, climbing, indoor and outdoor play, and animals.

transport.fromHackney: Piccadilly Line to Hounslow West, then a bus to Hobbledown Heath.
transport.tip: Allow a full day to explore everything the park has to offer.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: Roam around the gardens and find hidden Lindt Gold Bunny statues to win a chocolate treat. Explore historic rooms, gardens, and the palace grounds.
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor energy-burner. Dress for wind. Bring snacks. Let them run.

transport.fromHackney: Train from Liverpool Street to Hampton Court.
transport.tip: Pre-booking is advised!

activities.title: Polka Theatre: We're Going on a Bear Hunt
activities.description: A musical stage version of Michael Rosen's beloved book, full of audience participation and live music.
activities.whyItWorks: Audience participation and live music - exactly the kind of thing that keeps wiggly preschoolers engaged. Also has a café and buggy park

transport.fromHackney: District Line from Monument to Wimbledon.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance as it's a popular show.

activities.title: Paddington the Musical
activities.description: Opened to glowing reviews and has quickly become a family favourite.
activities.whyItWorks: Captivating sets, iconic songs, and a heartwarming story. A great introduction to musical theatre.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Covent Garden or Temple.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance for best availability.

activities.title: Peppa Pig's egg-stra special Easter Afternoon Tea Bus Tour
activities.description: Every day this Easter a Peppa Pig themed afternoon tea will be run with special Easter travel cup and Easter Egg hunt activity on a London bus.
activities.whyItWorks: Peppa Pig themed, afternoon tea, and Easter Egg hunt activity

transport.fromHackney: Various pick-up locations, check website for details.
transport.tip: Book in advance as it's a popular tour.

activities.title: A Squash and a Squeeze
activities.description: Heartwarming and hilarious adventure.
activities.whyItWorks: Puppet shows built for ages 2-5, and intimate space.

transport.fromHackney: Bus to Angel, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check for discounted tickets at 4.45pm on Thur, and at 4.45pm on 18 Mar. All tickets £10 at 4.45pm on Fri.
